description: Set parameters for a groundwater fluid flow analysis.

keywords.name: biot
keywords.description: The fluid-flow calculation uses the Biot coefficient, α, and the Biot modulus, M, if on. If off, then the fluid modulus, Kf, and porosity, n, are used and α = 1. The default is off. See Biot Coefficient and Biot Modulus.

keywords.name: cmodel
keywords.description: Associates a fluid constitutive model with specified zones in the grid. It also allows querying of properties and states of constitutive models.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-anisotropic
keywords.description: Assign the anisotropic flow model.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-isotropic
keywords.description: Assign the isotropic flow model.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-null
keywords.description: Assign the null fluid model.

keywords.name: cmodel-list
keywords.description: List the available fluid constitutive models and their properties.

keywords.name: fastflow
keywords.description: Turns the fastflow solution scheme on and off. See Fully Saturated Fast Flow.

keywords.name: fastflow-relaxation
keywords.description: Sets the relaxation parameters used in the fastflow solution scheme.

keywords.name: implicit
keywords.description: The implicit solution scheme in the fluid flow model is turned on or off. The default is off.

keywords.name: list-information
keywords.description: Outputs current configuration settings and status information for the fluid model.

keywords.name: list-property
keywords.description: Outputs the values of the fluid property s in all zones in the range.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Assigns properties for the fluid flow constitutive models of all zones in the rnage. Any number of name value pairs may be assigned. The type of the value a must match the type expected by the property s. Any constitutive model that has a matching property name will be assigned the value.

keywords.name: property-biot
keywords.description: Biot coefficient (grain compressibility), \(\alpha\). The default is 1.0.

keywords.name: property-porosity
keywords.description: Porosity, \(n\). The default is 0.5.

keywords.name: property-undrained-thermal-coefficient
keywords.description: Undrained thermal coefficient, \(\beta\).

keywords.name: permeability
keywords.description: Anisotropic Fluid Flow:

keywords.name: dip
keywords.description: Principal permeability plane dip angle (in degrees).

keywords.name: dip-direction
keywords.description: Principal permeability plane dip direction angle (in degrees).

keywords.name: permeability-1
keywords.description: Principal permeability value.

keywords.name: permeability-2
keywords.description: Principal permeability value.

keywords.name: permeability-3
keywords.description: Principal permeability value.

keywords.name: permeability-xx
keywords.description: xx-component permeability.

keywords.name: permeability-xy
keywords.description: xy-component permeability.

keywords.name: permeability-xz
keywords.description: xz-component permeability.

keywords.name: permeability-yy
keywords.description: yy-component permeability.

keywords.name: permeability-yz
keywords.description: yz-component permeability.

keywords.name: permeability-zz
keywords.description: zz-component permeability.

keywords.name: rotation
keywords.description: Principal permeability plane rotation angle (in degrees).

keywords.name: property-distribution
keywords.description: Assigns a single property name to the fluid flow constitutive models of all zones in the range. The property value may be varied in space by using the following optional keyword values.

keywords.name: property-distribution-add
keywords.description: Add a to the property value. This keyword only applies to float type property values.

keywords.name: property-distribution-deviation-gaussian
keywords.description: A gaussian distribution is used to assign the value randomly, with a mean value of a and a standard deviation of f. This keyword only applies to float type property values.

keywords.name: property-distribution-deviation-uniform
keywords.description: A uniform distribution is used to assign the value randomly, with a mean value of a and a standard deviation of f. This keyword only applies to float type property values.

keywords.name: property-distribution-gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the property value. This keyword only applies to float type property values.

keywords.name: property-distribution-multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the property value by a.

keywords.name: property-distribution-vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the property value. This keyword only applies to float type property values.

keywords.name: saturation-tolerance
keywords.description: The effect of saturation changes to gridpoints is only applied when the accumulated saturation change is greater than this value. This is done for efficiency of calculation. The default value is 1x10:exp:\(-3\)

keywords.name: time-total
keywords.description: Specifies the accumulated zone fluid time, defined as the sum of all the timesteps over which zone fluid is active. Once set, fluid time will continue to accumulate with subsequent cycles.

keywords.name: track-active
keywords.description: Turns particle tracking on or off. track is off by default.

keywords.name: track-create
keywords.description: Create one or more particles in zones. The location and number of the particles created are controlled by the following keywords. The <[zonefluidoptions]> following can be used to specify attributes on the created particles.

keywords.name: track-create-point
keywords.description: Place a particle in the grid at the specified location v. For each vector value given, another particle will be created.

keywords.name: track-create-line
keywords.description: Add particles to the grid along a line.

keywords.name: track-create-line-begin
keywords.description: Specify the start position of the line. The default value is (0,0,0).

keywords.name: track-create-line-end
keywords.description: Specify the end position of the line. The default value is (0,0,0).

keywords.name: track-create-line-segment
keywords.description: Specify the line be divided equally into i + 1 segments, causing i particles to be introduced into the grid. The default value is 2.

keywords.name: track-create-plane
keywords.description: Add particles to the grid along a plane defined by two lines.

keywords.name: track-create-plane-begin
keywords.description: Specifies the shared start point of both lines that will define the plane. The default value is (0,0,0).

keywords.name: track-create-plane-end-1
keywords.description: Specifies end of the first line. The default value is (0,0,0).

keywords.name: track-create-plane-end-2
keywords.description: Specifies end of the second line. The default value is (0,0,0).

keywords.name: track-create-plane-segment
keywords.description: Divides the first line into i1 + 1 equal segments; divides the second line into i2 + 1 equal segments. i1 x i2 particles will be introduced to the grid on an evenly spaced grid along the plane. The default value of i1 and i2 is 2.

keywords.name: track-delete
keywords.description: Delete particles within the given range.

keywords.name: track-group
keywords.description: Assign or remove groups to particles in the range. Use of the group logic is described in Group.

keywords.name: track-group-remove
keywords.description: Remove the group s from particles within the given range. This does not remove the particles themselves.

keywords.name: track-group-slot
keywords.description: Assign groups in the given range to the slot named s. By default groups will be assigned to slot Default

keywords.name: track-list
keywords.description: lists summary information about tracking particles. If the path keyword is omitted, the current particle position information is output. If the path keyword is included, then particle path information is output.

keywords.name: track-trace
keywords.description: Creates a location trace (see the Trace section) of the particle with ID number i.

keywords.name: zone-based-pp
keywords.description: Indicates that pore-pressures should be retrieved directly from the zone, rather than averaged from the gridpoints. Zone pore pressures must be assigned manually via FISH, using the zone.pp intrinsic.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created particles to the group s1 and (if supplied) assign that group to slot s2. By default the slot name will be Default.

keywords.name: tail
keywords.description: When on (the default setting), particles will automatically store a list of coordinates along their path as they move through the grid. If off particles will only keep track of their current position.

keywords.name: tortuosity
keywords.description: The tortuosity is specified as f, which is defined as the ratio of the straight line path to the actual fluid path through a zone. This factor will be used to multiply track increments calculated from the flow velocity; it defaults to 1 if not given.

keywords.name: biot
keywords.description: The fluid-flow calculation uses the Biot coefficient, \(α\), and the Biot modulus, \(M\), if on. If off, then the fluid modulus, \(K_f\), and porosity, \(n\), are used and \(α\) = 1. The default is off. See Biot Coefficient and Biot Modulus.

keywords.name: active
keywords.description: Set the fluid process on or off. The process is on for zones, by default, when the model configure fluid command is given. Specify off to suppress the fluid calculation for zones.

keywords.name: biot
keywords.description: Set use of the Biot coefficient. If on , the fluid-flow calculation uses the Biot coefficient, \(α\) , and the Biot modulus, \(M\) . If off , then the fluid modulus, \(K_f\) , and porosity, \(n\) , are used and \(α\) = 1. The default is off . See Biot Coefficient and Biot Modulus .

keywords.name: cmodel-list
keywords.description: Associate a fluid constitutive model with specified zones in the grid. The command also allows querying of properties and states of constitutive models. Assign a fluid model to all zones in the range . This keyword may only be given if the model is configured for fluid calculations (i.e., the command model configure fluid has been issued). Fluid models are described in Grid Configured for Fluid Flow . Assign the anisotropic flow model. Assign the isotropic flow model. Assign the null fluid model. List the available fluid constitutive models and their properties.

keywords.name: fastflow
keywords.description: Turn the fastflow solution scheme on/off . See Fully Saturated Fast Flow .

keywords.name: fastflow-relaxation
keywords.description: Set the relaxation parameters used in the fastflow solution scheme.

keywords.name: implicit-solver-pcg-iterations-limit-preserve
keywords.description: Turn implicit mode on/off . By default implicit mode is off . Implicit mode requires a manually specified timestep. If no keyword is specified, the implicit logic uses iterative Jacobi solver. If Jacobi solver fails to converge to the solution, the logic switches to preconditioned conjugate gradient solver (see below). The following keywords apply: Use the iterative Jacobi solver based on the local matrix formulation (see Implicit Formulation ). Note that specifying a timestep that is too large can cause the Jacobi solver to fail to converge. If this keyword is specified and the solver diverges, the system will exit with an error message. The benefit of using the Jacobi solver compared to other solvers is that it is usually faster for small implicit timesteps (<< the divergence timestep). Use the direct solver similar to matrix inversion (see Advanced implicit solvers ). This highly efficient solver is able to handle small to medium size models (< 50-100K zones) and it is unconditionally stable for any timestep. Before this solver is called, the global right-hand-side (RHS) vector and global coefficient matrix (M) are formed for the whole model to incorporate contributions from all relevant gridpoints. Then the solver solves a system of linear equations for unknown global vector of pore pressure changes. The following keyword is available: Specify to preserve the global pressure coefficients matrix and only update its diagonal containing timestep-dependent terms. In general, the global matrix M must be re-created each time the implicit solver is called as model parameters, such as geometry, physical properties, implicit timestep, etc., may change. However, if the model parameters do not change during the simulation but user wishes to specify a different implicit timestep (e.g., to gradually increase it every certain number of steps), only the diagonal terms of the global matrix must be updated, which is very efficient. To stop this procedure and form a new global pressure coefficients matrix, the command zone thermal implicit must be issued (this resets the implicit solvers settings). Use the preconditioned conjugate gradient iterative solver (see Advanced implicit solvers ). This highly efficient solver is able to handle very large models and it is unconditionally stable for any timestep due to its special technique of matching desired solution accuracy to the number of iterations required to reach this accuracy. Before the solver is called, the global right-hand-side (RHS) vector and global pressure coefficients matrix (M) are created for the whole model to incorporate contributions from all relevant gridpoints. Then the solver analyses matrix properties (eigenvalues, condition number) and determines the required number of iterations, after which the iterative solution procedure starts. The following keywords are available. Specify an error tolerance (solution accuracy) that will determine when to stop iterations. The default tolerance value is 1.e-6. Specify the limit on the number of iterations. By default, the number of iterations is determined based on the analysis of the global matrix properties, such as minimum/maximum eigenvalues and the the condition number. This analysis is usually done rather fast, and it assures that the system can be solved to reach desired solution accuracy. Specifying a limit on the number of iteration skips the global matrix analysis, which may decrease overall solution time, but the risk of this is that the number of iterations specified may be insufficient to reach desired accuracy. The pcg solver has a general limit of 1000 on the number of iterations. Specify to preserve the global pressure coefficients matrix and only update its diagonal containing timestep-dependent terms. For detailed description see the equivalent usage above with the solver-direct keyword.

keywords.name: list-property-rotation
keywords.description: Output current configuration settings and status information for the fluid model. Output the values of the specified fluid property in all zones in the range. Biot coefficient (grain compressibility), \(\alpha\) . The default is 1.0. List isotropic hydraulic conductivity, \(k\) . This is the conventional hydraulic conductivity in units of [Length/Time]. Non-zero gravity and water density should be set in advance. Hydraulic conductivity must be non-zero if the command zone fluid active on applies. Only one between hydraulic conductivity and permeability is needed. List porosity, \(n\) . The default is 0.5. List undrained thermal coefficient, \(\beta\) . List isotropic permeability, \(k\) . The permeability used in FLAC is defined as the conventional hydraulic conductivity divided by the unit weight of water. Permeability must be non-zero if the command zone fluid active on applies. Only one between hydraulic conductivity and permeability is needed. List the principal permeability plane dip angle (in degrees). List the principal permeability plane dip direction angle (in degrees). List the principal permeability value. List the principal permeability value. List the principal permeability value. List the xx -component of permeability. List the xy -component of permeability. List the xz -component of permeability. List the yy -component of permeability. List the yz -component of permeability. List the zz -component of permeability. List the principal permeability plane rotation angle (in degrees).

keywords.name: property-rotation
keywords.description: Assign properties for the fluid flow constitutive models of all zones in the range. Any number of name-value pairs may be assigned. The type of the value a must match the type expected by the property s . Any constitutive model that has a matching property name will be assigned the value. Set the biot coefficient (grain compressibility), \(\alpha\) . The default is 1.0. Set porosity, \(n\) . The default is 0.5. Set the undrained thermal coefficient, \(\beta\) . Isotropic Fluid Flow: Set isotropic hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Gravity and water density must be defined to use this quanitity. Only one of hydraulic-conductivity or permeability needs to be assigned. Set isotropic permeability, \(k\) . The permeability used in FLAC is defined as the conventional hydraulic permeability divided by the unit weight of water. Permeability must be non-zero if the command zone fluid active on applies. Only one of hydraulic-conductivity or permeability needs to be assigned. Anisotropic Fluid Flow: Set the principal permeability plane dip angle (in degrees). Set the principal permeability plane dip direction angle (in degrees). Set the principal hydraulic conductivity value (units of length/time). Set the principal hydraulic conductivity value (units of length/time). Set the principal hydraulic conductivity value (units of length/time). Set the xx -component of hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Set the xy -component of hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Set the xz -component of hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Set the yy -component of hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Set the yz -component of hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Set the zz -component of hydraulic conductivity (units of length/time). Set the principal permeability value. Set the principal permeability value. Set the principal permeability value. Set the xx -component of permeability. Set the xy -component of permeability. Set the xz -component of permeability. Set the yy -component of permeability. Set the yz -component of permeability. Set the zz -component of permeability. Set the principal permeability plane rotation angle (in degrees).

keywords.name: property-distribution-vary
keywords.description: Assign a single property name to the fluid flow constitutive models of all zones in the range. The property value may be varied in space by using the following optional keyword values. Add a to the property value. This keyword only applies to float type property values. A gaussian distribution is used to assign the value randomly, with a mean value of a and a standard deviation of f . This keyword only applies to float type property values. A uniform distribution is used to assign the value randomly, with a mean value of a and a standard deviation of f . This keyword only applies to float type property values. Apply a gradient to the property value. This keyword only applies to float type property values. Note the origin keyword is optional, however, the default value (0,0,0) is used if the origin keyword is not included in the command — which may not produce the intended result. Multiply the property value by a . Apply a linear variation to the property value. This keyword only applies to float type property values.

keywords.name: saturation-tolerance
keywords.description: Specify that the effect of saturation changes to gridpoints is only applied when the accumulated saturation change is greater than this value. This is done for efficiency of calculation. The default value is 1e-3.

keywords.name: time-total
keywords.description: Specify the accumulated zone fluid time, defined as the sum of all the timesteps over which zone fluid is active. Once set, fluid time will continue to accumulate with subsequent cycles.

keywords.name: track-trace
keywords.description: Place a particle in the grid at the location specified. The particle flows with the fluid and maintains a record of its path over a finite period of time. The grid must be configured for fluid flow (see model configure fluid ), and the zone must have a non-null fluid model in order for a particle to exist in a zone. The particle is not created (and no error message is output) if the location does not map into a zone with these specifications. The particles have no mass and do not influence the flow or mechanical behavior in any way; they are simply carried along by the fluid and record its motion. Several TRACK commands may be given, at different times, if desired. See the data files in One-Dimensional Filling of a Porous Region for an example of particle track usage. Location and keywords are given as follows. Turn particle tracking on/off . track is off by default. Create one or more particles in zones. The location and number of the particles created are controlled by the following keywords. The <[zonefluidblock]> following can be used to specify attributes on the created particles. Place a particle in the grid at the specified location v . For each vector value given, another particle will be created. Add particles to the grid along a line. Specify the start position of the line. The default value is (0,0,0). Specify the end position of the line. The default value is (0,0,0). Specify the line be divided equally into i + 1 segments, causing i particles to be introduced into the grid. The default value is 2. Add particles to the grid along a plane defined by two lines. Specify the shared start point of both lines that will define the plane. The default value is (0,0,0). Specify the end of the first line. The default value is (0,0,0). Specify the end of the second line. The default value is (0,0,0). Divide the first line into i1 + 1 equal segments and divide the second line into i2 + 1 equal segments. i1 x i2 particles will be introduced to the grid on an evenly spaced grid along the plane. The default values of i1 and i2 are 2. Delete particles within the given range . Assign or remove groups to particles in the range. Use of the group logic is described in Groups . Remove the group s from particles within the given range. This does not remove the particles themselves. Assign groups in the given range to the slot named s . By default groups will be assigned to slot Default . List summary information about tracking particles. If the path keyword is omitted, the current particle position information is output. If the path keyword is included, then particle path information is output. Create a location trace (see the trace command) of the particle with ID number i .

keywords.name: zone-based-pp
keywords.description: Specify that pore-pressures should be retrieved directly from the zone, rather than averaged from the gridpoints. Zone pore pressures must be assigned manually via FISH , using the zone.pp intrinsic.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the created particles to the group s1 and, if supplied, assign that group to slot s2 . By default the slot name will be Default .

keywords.name: tail
keywords.description: When on (the default setting), particles will automatically store a list of coordinates along their path as they move through the grid. If off particles will only keep track of their current position.

keywords.name: tortuosity
keywords.description: Specify the tortuosity as f . Tortuosity is the ratio of the straight line path to the actual fluid path through a zone. This factor will be used to multiply track increments calculated from the flow velocity. The value defaults to 1 if not given.

9.1.description: Set parameters for a groundwater fluid-flow analysis. FLAC 9.1 and later require model configure fluid-flow.

keywords.name: active
keywords.description: Set the zone fluid process on or off after model configure fluid-flow.

keywords.name: cmodel
keywords.description: Select, assign, or list zone fluid constitutive models.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign
keywords.description: Assign a fluid constitutive model to zones in the range. FLAC3D 9.x runtime accepts active, inactive, isotropic, and anisotropic.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-active
keywords.description: Assign the active fluid flow model. Runtime-validated in FLAC3D 9.x.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-inactive
keywords.description: Assign the inactive fluid model, making the zone effectively impermeable.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-isotropic
keywords.description: Assign the isotropic fluid flow model. Runtime-validated in FLAC3D 9.x.

keywords.name: cmodel-assign-anisotropic
keywords.description: Assign the anisotropic fluid flow model. Runtime-validated in FLAC3D 9.x.

keywords.name: cmodel-list
keywords.description: List available fluid constitutive models and properties.

keywords.name: conserve-generated
keywords.description: Redistribute generated pore pressure away from fixed-pore-pressure gridpoints during two-way coupling.

keywords.name: fastflow
keywords.description: Turn the fastflow solution scheme on or off.

keywords.name: fastflow-relaxation
keywords.description: Set relaxation parameters for the fastflow solution scheme.

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: Set the global fluid density used by the fluid-flow calculation.

keywords.name: implicit
keywords.description: Configure implicit fluid-flow solution mode.

keywords.name: implicit-maximum-iterations
keywords.description: Set the maximum matrix-solver iteration count for iterative implicit solvers.

keywords.name: implicit-maxnonlinear-iterations
keywords.description: Set the maximum nonlinear iterations when a saturation model is active.

keywords.name: implicit-servo
keywords.description: Configure the optional timestep servo for implicit fluid calculations.

keywords.name: implicit-solver
keywords.description: Select the matrix solver used during implicit fluid-flow mode.

keywords.name: implicit-tolerance
keywords.description: Set convergence tolerance for iterative implicit fluid solvers.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: Output fluid-flow configuration, model, and property information.

keywords.name: list-information
keywords.description: Output current configuration settings and status information for the fluid model.

keywords.name: list-property
keywords.description: Output the requested fluid property for zones in the range.

keywords.name: modulus-scale
keywords.description: Scale fluid or Biot modulus values for flow-only or coupled consolidation analysis.

keywords.name: modulus-scale-limit-modulus
keywords.description: Limit scaled fluid modulus values.

keywords.name: modulus-scale-limit-modulus-biot
keywords.description: Limit scaled Biot modulus values.

keywords.name: modulus-scale-limit-stiffness-ratio
keywords.description: Limit the ratio between fluid storage stiffness and mechanical confined stiffness.

keywords.name: modulus-scale-restore
keywords.description: Restore original fluid or Biot modulus after modulus scaling.

keywords.name: modulus-automatic
keywords.description: Set Biot modulus from current mechanical confined stiffness; default ratio is 20 if omitted.

keywords.name: permeability-saturation
keywords.description: Specify how effective permeability varies with average gridpoint saturation.

keywords.name: permeability-saturation-constant
keywords.description: Keep permeability unchanged by saturation.

keywords.name: permeability-saturation-s-shape
keywords.description: Scale permeability with an S-shaped saturation relation.

keywords.name: permeability-saturation-table
keywords.description: Scale permeability by a named user table keyed by average zone saturation; quote the table name in FLAC commands.

keywords.name: property
keywords.description: Assign one or more fluid property name-value pairs to zones in the range.

keywords.name: property-biot-coefficient
keywords.description: Set the Biot coefficient for zones.

keywords.name: property-biot-modulus
keywords.description: Set the Biot modulus for zones.

keywords.name: property-consolidation-coefficient
keywords.description: Set consolidation coefficient as an alternate way to specify storage behavior.

keywords.name: property-density-saturated
keywords.description: Control whether saturated density is used for total fluid density contribution.

keywords.name: property-effective-cutoff
keywords.description: Set the lower bound used for effective-stress pore pressure calculations.

keywords.name: property-fluid-density
keywords.description: Set zone-specific fluid density.

keywords.name: property-fluid-modulus
keywords.description: Set zone fluid modulus.

keywords.name: property-hydraulic-conductivity
keywords.description: Set isotropic hydraulic conductivity in length-per-time units.

keywords.name: property-hydraulic-conductivity-tensor
keywords.description: Set anisotropic hydraulic conductivity components.

keywords.name: property-mobility-coefficient
keywords.description: Set isotropic mobility coefficient, the preferred 9.1+ permeability-style property.

keywords.name: property-mobility-coefficient-tensor
keywords.description: Set anisotropic mobility coefficient components.

keywords.name: property-pore-pressure-generation
keywords.description: Enable or disable pore-pressure generation from mechanical volumetric strain.

keywords.name: property-porosity
keywords.description: Set zone porosity.

keywords.name: property-undrained-thermal-coefficient
keywords.description: Set the undrained thermal coefficient.

keywords.name: property-distribution
keywords.description: Assign a spatially varied fluid property distribution.

keywords.name: property-distribution-add
keywords.description: Add to a float property distribution value.

keywords.name: property-distribution-gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to a float property distribution value.

keywords.name: property-distribution-multiply
keywords.description: Multiply a property distribution value.

keywords.name: property-distribution-vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to a float property distribution value.

keywords.name: saturation-tolerance
keywords.description: Set the saturation-change threshold used before saturation effects are applied.

keywords.name: steady-state
keywords.description: Solve the steady-state fluid-flow system; unsaturated models use nonlinear Picard iteration.

keywords.name: steady-state-effective
keywords.description: Control whether effective stress or pore pressure is held consistent during steady-state updates.

keywords.name: steady-state-linear-first
keywords.description: Use a preliminary linear solution to accelerate convergence for cutoff unsaturated flow models.

keywords.name: steady-state-maximum-iterations
keywords.description: Set the Picard iteration limit for steady-state unsaturated solutions.

keywords.name: timestep
keywords.description: Manage zone fluid timestep controls.

keywords.name: timestep-automatic
keywords.description: Return fluid timestep selection to automatic mode.

keywords.name: timestep-fix
keywords.description: Fix the fluid timestep to a user-specified value. Runtime keyword is fix.

keywords.name: time-total
keywords.description: Set accumulated zone fluid time.

keywords.name: track
keywords.description: Create, delete, list, and group passive particles that track fluid flow paths.

keywords.name: track-active
keywords.description: Turn fluid particle tracking on or off.

keywords.name: track-create
keywords.description: Create passive fluid tracking particles.

keywords.name: track-delete
keywords.description: Delete fluid tracking particles in the range.

keywords.name: track-group
keywords.description: Assign or remove groups on fluid tracking particles.

keywords.name: track-list
keywords.description: List current particle locations or path information.

keywords.name: track-trace
keywords.description: Create a location trace for a fluid tracking particle.

keywords.name: unsaturated
keywords.description: Assign the unsaturated flow model used at gridpoints; assignment can vary spatially.

keywords.name: unsaturated-brooks-corey
keywords.description: Use the Brooks-Corey saturation-suction relation.

keywords.name: unsaturated-cutoff
keywords.description: Use a cutoff model that prevents pore pressure from falling below the specified tension.

keywords.name: unsaturated-gardner
keywords.description: Use the Gardner saturation-suction relation.

keywords.name: unsaturated-off
keywords.description: Disable unsaturated flow behavior.

keywords.name: unsaturated-table
keywords.description: Use a table to define the saturation-suction relation.

keywords.name: unsaturated-van-genuchten
keywords.description: Use the van Genuchten saturation-suction relation.

keywords.name: zone-based-pp
keywords.description: Use zone pore pressures directly rather than gridpoint-averaged pore pressures.

version_notes: ITASCA 9.1 and later use model configure fluid-flow before zone fluid commands; FLAC 9.0 used model configure fluid.
Unsaturated-flow and saturation-dependent permeability controls are documented in the 9.1+ zone fluid command page.
For 9.1+ mobility-coefficient and hydraulic-conductivity property names should be preferred over older permeability property names.
Runtime validation in FLAC3D 9.x accepted cmodel assign active/inactive/isotropic/anisotropic and timestep fix; cmodel assign linear and timestep fixed were rejected.

9.3.reason: FLAC 9.3+ uses the bundled 9.1 fluid-flow syntax block for this command until an exact 9.3 page is bundled.
